what_it_does: Implements the Session Supervisor service: a long-running local HTTP service that spawns, resumes, forks, injects turns into, interrupts, and tears down held-open Claude Code subprocesses; consumes mailbox intents from mail.jsonl; fans per-session stream-json events to subscribers; and enforces concurrency, timeout, permission, and single-writer event-log policies.
observations: Binds 127.0.0.1 only and deliberately provides no env var to widen exposure.
Is the sole writer of `agent_sessions.*` events to the shared events.jsonl.
Concurrency cap is read call-time from `COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY` and defaults to 3.
Per-turn wall-clock timeout is read call-time from `COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S` and defaults to 900s.
Uses held-open stdin with `--input-format stream-json` and `--output-format stream-json`.
Mailbox is `agent_sessions/mail.jsonl` consumed via a cursor ref `agent_sessions/cursor:supervisor`.
Mailbox verbs DELIVER, WAKE, and CONSULT route to inject, spawn/resume, or fork.
HTTP API exposes health, sessions, watch, spawn, inject, interrupt, teardown, and bridge-session endpoints.
RAIL R1-prime is a consent-gated `bridge-session` spawn profile with a wider tool allowlist for git/lsp/web but explicit refusal for computer/browser.
`SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY` maps body keys to flag emission kinds (bool/value/csv/repeat/swap) and teaching text; posture is derived from the Mirror-Registry rules.
`_provider_env` always returns `{}` because ollama routes via a launcher command, not env injection.
`_extract_usage` pulls cost/token usage from claude `result` events for durable turn records.
Per-session state machine is `starting -> idle <-> busy -> closed`.
standouts: The R1-prime consent-gated bridge-session profile with explicit host/rail boundaries.
Registry-derived spawn-flag posture replacing a hand-maintained posture column.
Held-open stream-json stdin-injection transport for live sessions.
Single-writer event-log law and concurrency cap enforced with teaching refusals.
conventions: Heavy module-level docstring and inline laws cite audit IDs (B3, C6, C3, C9, N4).
Call-time env reads for config so tests can monkeypatch without restart.
Policy breaches raise `TeachingRefusal` with explanatory remediation text.
Pure static helpers for command building to enable unit testing without real spawns.
Fleet mutations under `self.lock`, per-session stdin writes under `s.stdin_lock`.
Global cached `_PLATFORM_SIGNAL_SETS` loaded once from the platform registry.
concerns: File is very large and mixes HTTP server, subprocess management, mailbox loop, and spawn-flag registry in one module.
Extensive inline commentary may drift from the actual implementation over time.
`_provider_env` is named as if it might inject environment variables but always returns `{}`, which could mislead readers.
`_PLATFORM_SIGNAL_SETS` is cached without invalidation; registry updates require a process restart.
The ollama launcher special-case embeds provider-specific command construction inside the generic spawn builder.
Several methods are truncated in the provided excerpt, so full behavior cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: This is the fabric's session-orchestration spine: a local HTTP service that keeps Claude Code subprocesses alive and injects turns, wrapped in heavy policy commentary and a registry-driven spawn-flag classifier.
questions: How exactly does `_resolve_bridge_tools` combine `extra_tools` with capability-derived tools when the implementation is truncated?
How are render-declaration events integrated in `_declare_and_fan`?
How does the mailbox cursor handle compaction or truncation of `mail.jsonl`?
What is the exact JSON shape of the `control_request` sent by `interrupt`?
Besides `atexit`, how are SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers wired in `main`?
How does the service discover or validate its port against `services.json`?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the code: it is a local session-supervisor service that spawns/resumes/forks/injects/teardowns Claude Code sessions, consumes mailbox intents, and exposes an HTTP API; the implementation is organized around those responsibilities.
apparent_themes: Single writer / one owner per session.
Teaching refusals instead of silent failures.
Audit traceability via cited law IDs.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a policy-heavy, audit-citable, unit-testable session orchestrator that keeps Claude Code alive as a fabric substrate while refusing dangerous or misconfigured spawns loudly and teaching the operator how to proceed.
novelty: The registry-derived spawn-flag posture (no hand-maintained posture column) and the R1-prime consent-gated bridge-session profile with explicit host/rail boundaries for computer/browser.
contribution: A concrete, policy-annotated supervisor implementation for held-open Claude Code sessions, including a mailbox consumer, HTTP control surface, per-session event fan-out, timeout watchdog, concurrency cap, and a registry-driven spawn-flag classifier.
summary_for_embedding: A Python service module that supervises held-open Claude Code subprocesses via a local HTTP API, mailbox intent consumption, and policy-enforced spawn flags.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single-owner, audit-citable session fabric spine that injects user turns into live Claude Code sessions while enforcing concurrency, timeout, permission, and consent-gated capability boundaries.

symbols.does: Reads the live concurrency cap from COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY at call time.
symbols.description: Returns an integer defaulting to 3, following the call-time env pattern so tests can monkeypatch the cap without restarting the service.

symbols.does: Reads the live permission posture from COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION at call time.
symbols.description: Returns a string defaulting to `plan` (read-only), used as the default `--permission-mode` for supervised sessions unless overridden.

symbols.does: Reads the per-turn wall-clock timeout from COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S at call time.
symbols.description: Returns a float defaulting to 900 seconds; the watchdog uses this ceiling to reap sessions whose turn exceeds it.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO 8601 string.
symbols.description: Simple helper used to stamp events and session records.

symbols.does: Exception type for policy refusals that include explanatory teaching text.
symbols.description: Raised when a request would violate concurrency caps, locked spawn flags, or consent requirements; the message names the limit, live state, and remediation.

symbols.does: Loads and caches the claude-code PlatformEntry signal_sets from the platform registry.
symbols.description: Resolves the registry row once on first use and fails loudly if it is missing, because the registry is the sole source of spawn-flag posture truth.

symbols.does: Derives a spawn flag's posture from the Mirror-Registry rules.
symbols.description: Classifies a flag name against the cached claude-code signal sets, returning locked/hazard/consent/safe/unmatched; locked transport-invariant rules take precedence over consent and safe classifications.

symbols.does: Applies caller-supplied registry spawn flags to a command list with consent checks.
symbols.description: Validates each flag key against SPAWN_FLAG_ASSEMBLY, derives posture from the registry, refuses locked/hazard flags, requires consent for consent-posture flags, and emits bool/value/csv/repeat/swap style arguments onto the command.

symbols.does: Represents one supervisor-owned Claude Code subprocess and its state.
symbols.description: Holds the session id, name, cwd, resume/fork metadata, profile, state machine, subprocess handle, event deque, subscriber queues, and in-flight turn bookkeeping.

symbols.does: Initializes a new supervised session record in the `starting` state.
symbols.description: Generates a local handle, stores cwd/resume/fork/source, initializes activity timestamps, and prepares empty event and subscriber collections.

symbols.does: Returns a JSON-serializable snapshot of the session's state.
symbols.description: Includes id, claude_session_id, name, cwd, state, resume/fork flags, creation time, last activity, turn count, pid, close reason, and profile.

symbols.does: Checks whether a key matches the session's local or Claude session id.
symbols.description: Returns True if the key equals either `self.id` or `self.claude_session_id`.

symbols.does: Extracts cost and token usage from a claude result event.
symbols.description: Pulls `usage`, `model_usage`, `total_cost_usd`, and `model` fields into a flat dict, returning None when no usage or cost data is present so the durable turn event stays free of empty noise.

symbols.does: Owns and manages the fleet of supervised sessions.
symbols.description: Coordinates subprocess lifecycle, mailbox consumption, event fan-out, durable event writes, and HTTP request handling under a fleet lock and per-session stdin lock.

symbols.does: Initializes the supervisor with a filesystem store.
symbols.description: Sets up the session map, fleet lock, mailbox path, and stop event used by background loops.

symbols.does: Appends an agent_sessions event to the store's event log.
symbols.description: Durable writes are re-raised on failure because their loss changes behavior; non-durable writes are logged and swallowed so watchdog loops survive transient store errors.

symbols.does: Pushes a per-session event to its event deque and subscriber queues.
symbols.description: Stamps the event with a sequence number and timestamp, appends it to the session's bounded history, and enqueues it for every `/watch` subscriber.

symbols.does: Returns the list of sessions whose state is not `closed`.
symbols.description: Helper used by the concurrency cap check to count currently owned sessions.

symbols.does: Raises TeachingRefusal if adding sessions would exceed the live cap.
symbols.description: Compares `len(live) + adding` against `fabric_concurrency()` and names the cap, env var, live sessions, and how to free a slot.

symbols.does: Purely builds the claude command list for a supervised spawn.
symbols.description: Constructs the held-open transport head (`-p`, `--input-format stream-json`, `--output-format`, `--verbose`, permission mode, strict MCP config, `mcp__company` allowlist) plus optional model, effort, fallback, settings, add_dir, partial messages, debug, safe-mode, bare, resume/fork, and ollama launcher handling; unit-testable without spawning.

symbols.does: Returns an empty environment overlay for the child process.
symbols.description: Stub that intentionally injects no env variables because ollama routes via a launcher command; kept so future env-only providers have a home.

symbols.does: Resolves bridge-session capabilities and extra tools into an allowlist.
symbols.description: Maps named capabilities (git/lsp/web/edit) to concrete tool names and refuses host/rail-boundary capabilities like computer/browser with teaching reasons; pure and unit-testable.

symbols.does: Builds the command for a consent-gated bridge-session spawn.
symbols.description: Assembles a wider `--allowedTools` list (Bash, Edit, Read, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, WebSearch plus mcp__company), sets `acceptEdits` permission mode by default, and applies resume/fork/model/effort/fallback/settings/add_dir as requested.

symbols.does: Spawns a new supervised Claude Code session.
symbols.description: Checks the cap, builds the command (floor profile or registry flags), starts the subprocess, begins stderr drain and stdout reader threads, optionally waits for init, and emits a durable spawned event.

symbols.does: Spawns a consent-gated bridge-session with wider capabilities.
symbols.description: Requires `operator_consent`, resolves requested capabilities and extra tools, refuses unavailable computer/browser, and delegates to `spawn` with the bridge-session profile.

symbols.does: Drains the session's stderr into a bounded tail.
symbols.description: Background thread that reads stderr lines so the subprocess pipe does not block; keeps the last ~50 lines for close diagnostics.

symbols.does: Declares and fans a claude stream event according to R1.2.
symbols.description: Wraps a raw claude emit with placement/component/field metadata and fans it as a `declared` event; undeclared or fallback emits trigger the drop hook.

symbols.does: Reads stream-json output from a session and fans events.
symbols.description: Long-running thread that parses stdout lines into events, updates session state (idle/busy), accumulates turn text, detects turn completion, and calls `_turn_done`.

symbols.does: Finalizes a completed or interrupted turn.
symbols.description: Stamps usage, emits a durable `agent_sessions.turn` event, appends a mailbox reply if the turn answered an intent, and transitions the session back to idle.

symbols.does: Looks up a session by local id or claude session id.
symbols.description: Returns the matching `Supervised` instance or None.

symbols.does: Injects a user turn into a live session's stdin.
symbols.description: Acquires the session's stdin lock, writes a stream-json user message, records the turn source/intent and start time, and transitions state to busy.

symbols.does: Sends a control request to interrupt the current turn.
symbols.description: Writes a stream-json `control_request` to the session's stdin to stop an in-flight assistant response.

symbols.does: Closes a session and terminates its subprocess.
symbols.description: Sets the terminal state, records the close reason, kills the process if requested, and emits a durable closed event.

symbols.does: Tears down a single owned session.
symbols.description: Public method that closes the session and removes it from the fleet map.

symbols.does: Tears down every owned session.
symbols.description: Iterates the fleet and closes all sessions, used at exit/SIGTERM to prevent orphan subprocesses.

symbols.does: Background loop that reaps sessions exceeding the turn timeout.
symbols.description: Polls live sessions, and if a busy session's turn has exceeded `turn_timeout_s()` with no progress, closes it with a timeout reason.

symbols.does: Reads the supervisor's mailbox cursor offset.
symbols.description: Returns the consumed byte offset from the store's `CURSOR_REF`, defaulting to 0 if absent.

symbols.does: Builds the reply thread identifier for a mailbox response.
symbols.description: Returns a string used to correlate the reply with the original intent thread.

symbols.does: Appends a successful mailbox reply for a completed intent.
symbols.description: Writes a reply event to `mail.jsonl` referencing the intent id and thread, with the assistant text as body.

symbols.does: Appends an error mailbox reply for a failed intent.
symbols.description: Writes an error event to `mail.jsonl` naming the intent and reason.

symbols.does: Retrieves the body text of a mailbox intent from the store.
symbols.description: Looks up the intent's `cas` ref and returns its content.

symbols.does: Background loop that consumes mailbox intents in order.
symbols.description: Polls `mail.jsonl` from the cursor, handles deliver/wake/consult verbs, blocks the cursor on mid-turn targets, and retries them next poll.

symbols.does: Processes one batch of mailbox records starting at the given offset.
symbols.description: Reads new lines, dispatches each intent, and returns the new cursor offset.

symbols.does: Determines a safe cwd when resuming a session from a mailbox intent.
symbols.description: Returns the target cwd or None if the resume target cannot be safely located.

symbols.does: Resolves the working directory for a mailbox intent target.
symbols.description: Chooses between explicit cwd and resume-derived cwd, returning None when unavailable.

symbols.does: Handles a single mailbox intent record.
symbols.description: Routes DELIVER/WAKE/CONSULT verbs to inject, spawn, or fork logic, and returns whether the cursor may advance.

symbols.does: Attempts to spawn or resume a session for a mailbox intent.
symbols.description: Handles the launch-time path for wake/consult intents, including cwd resolution and error replies.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for the supervisor's local API.
symbols.description: Subclass of `BaseHTTPRequestHandler` that dispatches GET/POST routes, serves health/sessions/watch, and accepts spawn/inject/interrupt/teardown/bridge-session commands.

symbols.does: Suppresses default HTTP access logging.
symbols.description: Overrides `BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message` to do nothing, keeping the service quiet.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON HTTP response.
symbols.description: Writes the status code, Content-Type header, and JSON-encoded body.

symbols.does: Dispatches GET requests to health, sessions, or watch endpoints.
symbols.description: Parses path and query string and routes to the appropriate handler or returns 404.

symbols.does: Streams ndjson events for a specific session.
symbols.description: Replays buffered events and then enqueues live events from the session's subscriber queue until the connection closes.

symbols.does: Dispatches POST requests to spawn, inject, interrupt, teardown, or bridge-session.
symbols.description: Reads JSON body, routes to the corresponding `SessionSupervisor` method, and returns results or teaching refusals.

symbols.does: Entry point that starts the supervisor HTTP service.
symbols.description: Parses optional port argument, creates the `FsStore` and `SessionSupervisor`, registers atexit/signal teardown handlers, starts background threads, and serves on 127.0.0.1.

symbols.does: Exit handler that tears down all sessions.
symbols.description: Nested function registered with atexit and signal handlers to terminate every owned subprocess on shutdown.
